chunk_summary: This section discusses fasting avoidance strategies and emergency feeding protocols for managing organic acidemias and fatty acid oxidation disorders, emphasizing therapeutic dietary interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of fasting avoidance in metabolic disorders like organic acidemias.
Learn emergency feeding protocols for patients with fatty acid oxidation disorders.
Identify key dietary strategies to prevent metabolic decompensation.
Recognize the role of therapeutic diets in managing inherited metabolic conditions.
key_facts: Fasting can trigger metabolic crises in patients with organic acidemias and fatty acid oxidation disorders.
Emergency feeding protocols often include glucose-based solutions to prevent catabolism.
Patients with fatty acid oxidation disorders may require frequent meals to maintain energy balance.
Therapeutic diets are tailored to individual metabolic needs to prevent decompensation.
Early intervention during illness or fasting is critical to avoid severe complications.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Fasting avoidance and emergency feeding protocols are critical in preventing metabolic crises in patients with organic acidemias and fatty acid oxidation disorders.
